_note: MOCK procedure result for PT-017. No real patient. For PreChart development/eval.

procedure.name: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy (EGD) with variceal band ligation
procedure.indication: Variceal screening in NASH cirrhosis (Child-Pugh B); single episode of melena ~2 weeks prior raising concern for possible prior variceal bleed
procedure.sedation: Monitored anesthesia care (anesthesiology-directed propofol) given Child-Pugh B cirrhosis, thrombocytopenia, possible hepatic encephalopathy, and documented history of severe PONV / delayed emergence with prior anesthesia
procedure.prep_quality: N/A (upper endoscopy) — NPO status adequate; clear oropharynx

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy with esophageal variceal band ligation. INDICATION: variceal screening in NASH cirrhosis (Child-Pugh B); reported single melenic stool ~2 weeks prior. PRE-PROCEDURE: Platelets 88k and INR 1.4 reviewed and attributed to cirrhotic synthetic dysfunction/hypersplenism, not anticoagulation; patient on no prescription antiplatelet/anticoagulant (OTC fish oil only, not held). Anesthesia-directed MAC used given comorbidity and prior adverse anesthesia history. SEDATION: MAC (propofol), well tolerated. FINDINGS: The esophagus demonstrated three columns of medium-to-large (grade 2-3) esophageal varices extending from the mid-esophagus to the gastroesophageal junction, with red wale marks on two columns indicating high risk of bleeding. No active bleeding; a small pigmented (flat, healed) area suggestive of a prior bleeding stigma was noted near the GE junction without active oozing. Four endoscopic band ligations were applied to the larger, high-risk columns with good deployment. The stomach showed a mosaic mucosal pattern consistent with mild-to-moderate portal hypertensive gastropathy; no gastric varices, no ulcer, no active bleeding. The examined duodenum to the second portion was normal. IMPRESSION: Medium-to-large esophageal varices with high-risk stigmata (red wale marks) and a healed bleeding stigma, consistent with the reported prior melena; four bands placed. Portal hypertensive gastropathy. Plan for repeat EGD banding sessions until variceal eradication and initiation of non-selective beta-blocker.

findings.finding: Grade 2-3 esophageal varices (3 columns) with red wale marks (high-risk stigmata)
Flat pigmented spot suggestive of healed/prior bleeding stigma, no active oozing

disposition: Discharged home same day in stable condition with escort after uneventful recovery. Post-band-ligation precautions reviewed: soft diet advanced as tolerated, avoid NSAIDs, return immediately for hematemesis, melena, chest pain, or dysphagia. Given healed bleeding stigma and high-risk varices, counseled on urgent return precautions and provided direct GI/hepatology contact.

followup.recommendation: Schedule repeat EGD for serial band ligation until variceal eradication (high-risk varices with evidence of prior bleed)
Initiate non-selective beta-blocker (e.g., carvedilol) for secondary prophylaxis, titrate as tolerated given ascites/blood pressure; coordinate with hepatology
Reconcile spironolactone dose — patient self-reduced to 50 mg daily; reassess ascites control and electrolytes with hepatology
Add hepatic encephalopathy to problem list (May 2026 episode); continue rifaximin 550 mg BID
Continue HCC surveillance imaging per cirrhosis protocol
Confirm no prescription anticoagulant/antiplatelet — OTC fish oil only, no bridging or resumption needed

planted_followup_items.description: High-risk esophageal varices (grade 2-3, red wale marks) with a healed bleeding stigma corroborating the reported prior melena — a bleeding source in a Child-Pugh B cirrhotic; must not be dropped as it drives secondary prophylaxis and urgent surveillance
planted_followup_items.expected_action: ensure hepatology/GI informed, patient counseled on urgent return precautions, and secondary-prophylaxis plan initiated

planted_followup_items.description: Repeat EGD for serial variceal band ligation until eradication
planted_followup_items.expected_action: schedule follow-up banding session in 2-4 weeks and track through eradication

planted_followup_items.description: Initiate non-selective beta-blocker (carvedilol) for secondary prophylaxis of variceal bleeding, coordinated with hepatology
planted_followup_items.expected_action: start and titrate NSBB; document blood pressure/ascites tolerance

planted_followup_items.description: Portal hypertensive gastropathy noted — reinforces portal hypertension severity; relevant to future anemia/bleeding workup
Spironolactone dose discrepancy — chart 100 mg vs patient-reported 50 mg; reconcile and reassess ascites/electrolytes
planted_followup_items.expected_action: reconcile actual dose with hepatology and adjust diuretic plan

planted_followup_items.description: Continue HCC surveillance imaging per cirrhosis protocol
planted_followup_items.expected_action: confirm next surveillance imaging is scheduled q6 months

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored. SYNTHETIC.

contacts.verbatim: My throat's sore and it feels a little tight in my chest when I swallow, like the food sticks for a second.
contacts.clinical_signal: Mild odynophagia/dysphagia and retrosternal discomfort day 1 after esophageal band ligation — expected post-banding symptom from band sites; no fever, no bleeding, no drooling/obstruction
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; transient sore throat, mild chest tightness and short-lived food-sticking are expected after band ligation. Advise soft/liquid diet, chew well, take meals slowly, sit upright, and use prescribed PPI/sucralfate. Return for high fever, severe or worsening chest pain, inability to swallow saliva, vomiting blood, or black stools.
contacts.rationale: TRIAGE TRAP (dangerous-sounding, benign): 'chest tightness on swallowing' after banding sounds alarming but is a common self-limited post-band symptom; escalate only if accompanied by red-flag features (bleeding, obstruction, fever, severe pain).

contacts.verbatim: My daughter buys me the fish oil pills she calls my blood thinner. I stopped them for the test — should I start taking them again now, and is there a new heart pill I'm supposed to begin?
contacts.clinical_signal: Medication reconciliation question — OTC omega-3 fish oil (mislabeled 'blood thinner', not a true anticoagulant, never required holding) plus query about starting the planned non-selective beta-blocker for variceal secondary prophylaxis
contacts.expected_action: Answer/reconcile: the fish oil is an OTC supplement, not a prescription blood thinner, and never needed to be held — she may resume it, and it poses no post-band bleeding concern. Confirm the carvedilol (NSBB) start/titration plan with GI/hepatology and how to take it given blood pressure/ascites.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the chart and follow-up plan; links to the beta-blocker initiation item (F3) and closes the fish-oil 'blood thinner' confusion — no true anticoagulant was ever involved.

contacts.verbatim: I threw up some blood a little while ago and my stool this morning was black and tarry again. I feel weak and dizzy when I stand up.
contacts.clinical_signal: Hematemesis plus melena with orthostatic lightheadedness ~11 days post-banding — classic timing for post-band ulcer bleeding / recurrent variceal hemorrhage in a Child-Pugh B cirrhotic with high-risk varices
contacts.expected_action: Escalate immediately for emergency evaluation (call 911 / ED activation); anticipate urgent EGD for hemostasis, resuscitation, transfusion as needed, and hepatology/GI notification. Do not manage as outpatient.
contacts.rationale: Post-band ulcer bleed/rebleed with active hematemesis, melena, and a hemodynamic symptom is a life-threatening red-flag complication in a cirrhotic with known high-risk varices (F1) requiring emergent action.
